patches_applied.patch: Footnote (i) in SEC-4.2.7a: replaced hand-wavy 'weakly non-linear...kill unaffected' (51w) with exact two-reading analysis — wholesale rescaling (requires h_Moon=246,401 km AND h_Sun=557,466 km) vs partial scaling (diverges, h_Moon=5,660 km within 73 km of firmament Sun). Conclusion upgraded: 'unaffected under linear charity, strengthened under exact geometry' (155w). Closes ISS-1643 (EXP-260.c1 H4).

One-clause parenthetical inserted in §4.2.7a 'Even more constrained' subsection after 'Coimbra–San Pablo at Δr=150 km' claim, cross-referencing P1 robustness paragraph and giving R_disc/180 values (75 km, 82 km, max-2 softening). Closes ISS-1747 (EXP-276.c2 H4).

Replaced 'unpublished' ΔB premise claim with accurate reference to dome's published W004 baseline (-22.24 nT, ~1.5× Kim & Chang ensemble upper edge, single-event derivation). Reframes vise: 'published but anomalous' beats 'unpublished and unverifiable'. Both horns still lose at least one WIN.

Replaced understated defender example (10-13 nT sub-range → 6.0-7.8 µGal) with honest ensemble upper bound (15 nT → 8.94 µGal, ~1 µGal short of 10 µGal floor, within ±2 µGal station uncertainty). Acknowledges dome's strongest retreat without conceding the argument.

Three-for-one: (H3) 'inversely' removed — Δg×κ=ΔB is the same coupling rearranged; (H4) 'named target stations' → 'named magnetometer stations along the August 2026 eclipse path'; (H5) ~80-word nested sentence split into 'Applying κ...' claim + arithmetic parenthetical.
